etymology.description: Early: 人(人) (rén, "person") + 爿(爿) (pán, "bed") -> person lying on bed -> Added 目(目) (mù, "eye") with emphasis -> Bronze: Added 夕(夕) (xī, "night") beneath -> Modern: 目 evolved into 罒, person became 冖, with 夕 retained
etymology.interpretation: The character 夢(夢) (mèng, "dream") evolved from a depiction of a person resting at night, conveying the concept of dreams occurring during sleep. The phono-semantic element 瞢 provides the pronunciation while 夕 emphasizes the nighttime context.
etymology.simplification: In 梦(夢), 目(目) (mù, "eye") was replaced with a simpler form in 罒, and the central complexity was reduced.

examples.chinese: 昨晚我做了一个奇怪的梦(昨晚我做了一個奇怪的夢)。
examples.pinyin: Zuówǎn wǒ zuòle yī gè qíguài de mèng.
examples.english: I had a strange dream last night.

examples.chinese: 她的梦想(她的夢想)是成为一名医生(是成為一名醫生)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de mèngxiǎng shì chéngwéi yī míng yīshēng.
examples.english: Her dream is to become a doctor.

examples.chinese: 无论多么艰难(無論多麼艱難)，我们都不应该放弃我们的梦想(我們都不應該放棄我們的夢想)。
examples.pinyin: Wúlùn duōme jiānnán, wǒmen dōu bù yīnggāi fàngqì wǒmen de mèngxiǎng.
examples.english: No matter how difficult it is, we should not give up our dreams.
